programme.title: Better Call Saul
programme.desc: Gus attempts to smooth things over with the cartel while Mike ties up loose ends north of the border.

programme.title: Better Call Saul
programme.desc: A lost dog leads to a new friend. A new player gets in the game.

programme.title: Better Call Saul
programme.desc: Emboldened by recent successes, the partners escalate their enterprise to new levels.

programme.title: The Guardian
programme.desc: As Nick represents an Arab American who is being sued for defending his restaurant against a vandal's attack, Burton must face a flurry of possible defections from the firm, including his own son.

programme.title: The Guardian
programme.desc: As Nick tries to reunite a twelve-year-old boy with a schizophrenic dad who murdered his wife, Alvin represents his ex-wife in a lawsuit being argued by Burton.

programme.title: The Guardian
programme.desc: Moving to a new law firm starts Nick him on a downhill slide; Alvin represents the daughter of a woman he regrets defending years ago.

programme.title: The Guardian
programme.desc: Nick suspects a longtime client of sexually abusing his daughter.

programme.title: The Guardian
programme.desc: As Nick takes responsibility for a troubled teenage cousin, Burton is forced to confront some unpleasant memories of his late wife.

programme.title: The Guardian
programme.desc: A drug addicted mother takes her baby hostage in an effort to keep the infant from being taken away.

programme.title: The Guardian
programme.desc: As James represents a nephew being tried for the shooting death of his mom's abusive boyfriend, Nick's plans to make his feelings about Lulu clear are sidetracked by some unexpected arrivals.

programme.title: The Guardian
programme.desc: Nick and Lulu square off over the adoption that will split up two brothers; Jake struggles to find a way to keep his parents from losing their house.

programme.desc: As Nick represents an Arab American who is being sued for defending his restaurant against a vandal's attack, Burton must face a flurry of possible defections from the firm, including his own son.
